content.problemStatement:
You have a Greenhouse of height xxx units (i.e plants with height > xxx cannot be kept inside it) and nnn plants whose initial heights are
h1,h2…,hnh_1, h_2…,h_nh1​,h2​…,hn​. These plants are of a special kind: they grow by 1 unit if watered. You have to keep these plants in the greenhouse for at most ddd days. On the  iii’th day, you have to water exactly aia_iai​ different plants. You need to output the maximum number of days you can keep all the plants together inside the greenhouse (output ddd if answer > ddd). Of course, you shouldn't water a plant if it has reached the height of xxx.

###Input:


The first line contains the number of test cases TTT.
For each test case:
The first line contains xxx, nnn and ddd.
The next line contains nnn integers denoting h1,h2..hnh_1, h_2 .. h_nh1​,h2​..hn​.
The next line contains ddd integers denoting a1,a2..ada_1, a_2 .. a_da1​,a2​..ad​.
###Output:
For each test case, output a single integer denoting the maximum number of days for which you can keep the plants in the greenhouse.

###Constraints


1≤T≤51 \leq T \leq 51≤T≤5
1≤x,d,n,hi≤1051 \leq x, d, n, h_i  \leq 10^51≤x,d,n,hi​≤105
1≤ai≤n1 \leq a_i \leq n1≤ai​≤n
It is guaranteed that hi<xh_i < xhi​<x initially.
###Sample Input:
1
5 3 7
1 1 2
3 2 1 1 2 1 3

###Sample Output:
6

###Explanation:
Here is a possible answer. These are the heights of plants in different days -

Day 0: 1 1 2

Day 1: 2 2 3

Day 2: 3 3 3

Day 3: 3 3 4

Day 4: 3 4 4

Day 5: 4 4 5

Day 6: 4 5 5

On the 7th day the heights will be 5 6 6 so you can’t keep all the plants together. You can check that no other way of watering the plants will do any better. Hence the output is 6.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains the number of test cases T. For each test case: The first line contains three integers x, n, and d, where x is the height of the greenhouse, n is the number of plants, and d is the number of days. The next line contains n integers denoting the initial heights of the plants. The next line contains d integers denoting the number of plants to be watered on each day.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single integer denoting the maximum number of days for which you can keep the plants in the greenhouse.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The plants grow as follows: Day 0: 1 1 2, Day 1: 2 2 3, Day 2: 3 3 3, Day 3: 3 3 4, Day 4: 3 4 4, Day 5: 4 4 5, Day 6: 4 5 5. On Day 7, the plants would grow to 5 6 6, which exceeds the greenhouse height.

solution.approach: Simulate the process of watering the plants day by day. On each day, water the plants that have not reached the maximum height. If a plant reaches the maximum height, do not water it. The simulation stops when any plant reaches or exceeds the height of the greenhouse.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * d * n) — where T is the number of test cases, d is the number of days, and n is the number of plants.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) — for storing the heights of the plants.
